name: Terence Blanchard
abstract: Terence Oliver Blanchard (born March 13, 1962) is an American jazz trumpeter and composer. He has also written two operas and more than 80 film and television scores.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: People like Terence Blanchard, who I mentioned before. Ralph Peterson was a student there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: In addition to the quintet, we added Terence Blanchard on trumpet on two cuts
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: he and I played together with Terence Blanchard and Donald Harrison.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: David Torkanowsky
about_quotes.evidence: played with Terence Blanchard one time or two, a really great New Orleans drummer. He was going out for a month with Monty. And I said well just send me somebody good. And he sent one of his students, a brilliant drummer named Simon Lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Torkanowsky interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: if you have a Kenny Barron or if you have a Wynton Marsalis or whoever you have, a Terence Blanchard or whoever you have on your staff as part of your faculty people are attracted to that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: There is no direct interaction described between Jason Marsalis and Terence Blanchard. Blanchard is only mentioned in the context of a student (Brice Winston) who later plays with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
